title: Enabling ISAC on Low-Cost Devices via Spatial Channel Estimation with a Single RF Chain
authors: Jonguk Lee, Hayoung Gu, Jeongsik Choi
abstract: Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) is increasingly required in next-generation wireless systems, but conventional implementations relying on phased arrays and multiple RF chains are impractical for low-cost or power-constrained devices. This paper proposes a practical ISAC framework that operates with a single RF chain without requiring any modification to legacy OFDM-based standards such asWi-Fi. The key idea is to acquire full channel state information (CSI) from the preamble using a reference antenna, while obtaining only partial CSI from sparsely embedded pilot subcarriers during data transmission as the RF chain switches across antennas. Based on this mixture of full and partial CSI, multipath channel parameters are estimated via an iterative maximum likelihood framework, enabling joint extraction of delay and direction-of-arrival (DOA) information and accurate reconstruction of the full spatial channel. Simulation results show that the proposed method achieves high-resolution sensing performance and maintains effective SNR close to the full-CSI upper bound, despite relying on sparse pilot measurements and a single RF chain. These results demonstrate the feasibility of ISAC operation for low-cost devices with constrained RF hardware.

title: Exploring Pre-Service Teachers’ Intention to Use Artificial Intelligence in Education: A Structural Equation Modeling Approach Based on UTAUT2
authors: Juan José Victoria Maldonado, Santiago Alonso García, Alejandro Martínez Menendez, Manuel Enrique Lorenzo Martín
publish: IEEE Revista Iberoamericana de Tecnologias del Aprendizaje
abstract: The growing relevance of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in education necessitates a better understanding of its acceptance among future educators. This study investigates the factors influencing pre-service teachers' intention to use AI, employing the UTAUT2 model extended with sociodemographic moderators. A cross-sectional quantitative design was applied to a sample of 908 undergraduate students from Early Childhood and Primary Education programs in Andalusia, Spain. Structural equation modeling results reveal that performance expectancy, effort expectancy, and habitual use are significant predictors of behavioral intention toward AI use. In contrast, the proposed moderating effects of gender and academic year were found to be non-significant. Findings highlight the pivotal role of habitual engagement with AI while questioning the effectiveness of current curricular approaches in promoting its pedagogical use, as academic progression showed no moderating influence. The study emphasizes the need for targeted training and curricular updates to foster meaningful AI integration in teacher education.

title: FilmShots: A Fast Film Shot Boundary Detection Based on Dilated Conv3D and Attention
authors: Chunfang Li, Kun Wang, Yuchen Pei, Yalv Fan, Zhetao Guo, Yushi Shen, Tongtong Zheng, Yuhe Hu, Fei Zhang
abstract: Shot boundary detection (SBD) is one of the representative tasks for computational film studies. Unlike television and short videos, movies present potential challenges, including dark scene, internal subtitles, occlusions, quick motions, flashes, color shifts, and low-resolution. This paper introduces a training data generation approach using random shot lengths to increase the positive samples and provides a 100-min public test set from 10 films. We propose a new end-to-end network FilmShots, which integrates temporal dilated convolutions to achieve a longer receptive field and attention mechanisms to enhance algorithm accuracy. The approach separately employs three techniques: Non-Local blocks (FilmShotNL) to capture long-range dependencies, Discrete Cosine Transform (FilmShotNLDCT) to reduce the computational complexity of spatiotemporal Non-Local blocks, and Conv1D (FilmShotECA) to compute channel attention weights. Additionally, this paper enhances interpretability by visualizing receptive fields of dilated Conv3D and attention operation. Experiments demonstrate that FilmShots surpasses both TransNetV2 and AutoShot in F1 scores, achieving the best performance currently available.

title: Incomplete Multimodal Federated Learning via Masking and Contrasting Prototypes_supp1-3658522.pdf
authors: D. Miao
abstract: In real-world scenarios, random modality missingness in multimodal federated learning (mFL) poses a significant challenge, diminishing the performance of global model inference. However, existing mFL methods are predominantly limited to simple scenarios that typically involve participant clients restricted to either a single modality or multimodal clients with complete modalities. They employ modality-specific encoders on each client and train modality fusion modules on the server, leading to severe task drift between clients and server, and struggling to generalize effectively in intricate modality-missing scenarios. To this end, we present a novel mFL framework to alleviate the task drift and performance degradation resulting from modality missingness during both training and inference. Inspired by prototype learning using the highly generalized proxy of specific information, we elaborately construct a prototype library to enhance FedAvg-based federated learning (FL). Naturally, we utilize prototypes as masks representing missing modalities to compensate for the missingness of modality information, formulating a task-calibrated training loss and devising a model-agnostic modality-incomplete inference strategy. In addition, a proximal term based on prototype contrastive learning is constructed to integrate interclient global information into each client, therefore enhancing local training. We conduct extensive experiments to evaluate our mFL framework, demonstrating its state-of-the-art performance across a series of missingness settings. Specifically, compared with existing mFL methods, our mFL framework improves inference performance under different modality missingness rates during training and by 23.8% during modality-incomplete inference.

title: Simultaneous Reflection and Transmission in Metasurfaces: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Ahmed Ali Umar, Farid Zubir, Mohd Fairus Mohd Yusoff, Muhammad Inam Abbasi, Shaharil Mohd Shah, Arshad Karimbu Vallappil, Maher Assaad, Noorlindawaty Md Jizat
abstract: The ability of metasurfaces to manipulate electromagnetic waves has drawn much attention especially with its ability to implement multifunctional structures such as simultaneous reflection and transmission. This paper discusses the fundamental principles and key design metrics such as bandwidth performance, efficiency, polarization control, frequency, and angular stability, which describe performance capabilities and limitations of metasurfaces. A wide operational bandwidth is indeed a requisite for multiple applications; while efficiency allows for energy efficient operations and optimal throughput. Additionally, polarization control and angular stability improves the performance of the metasurface, especially for dynamic environments and next generation communication systems. However, these metasurfaces have some design limitations; for example, trade-off between bandwidth and angular stability, and independent control of reflected and transmitted waves. This paper outlines these challenges and offers avenues for further innovation toward fully realizing the potential of metasurfaces in next-generation technologies.

title: Quantum Federated Gradient Aggregation using the Parameter-Shift Rule
authors: Jaehyun Chung, Chaemoon Im, Soohyun Park, Joongheon Kim, Wonjun Lee
abstract: Spurred by the limited availability of quantum resources, known as qubits, in recent quantum computers, quantum federated learning (QFL) is drawing attention. Due to its ability to fully utilize distributed qubits, QFL is suitable for developing quantum algorithms. QFL achieves local quantum gradients using the parameter-shift rule (P-S rule) and aggregates them, effectively coping with the limited number of qubits in each quantum computer. However, realizing QFL remains challenging due to the characteristics of the P-S rule, which requires two forward passes to compute the quantum gradients in each local quantum computer. These challenges become even more severe when the aggregation of the local quantum gradients occurs under heterogeneous channel conditions and data distributions. Motivated by this, this paper proposes the joint P-S rule, which eliminates the aggregation process in QFL and instead directly achieves the global quantum gradients. Furthermore, this paper proposes joint efficient quantum federated learning (Joint EQFL) that leverages successive interference cancellation and divergence-based clustering for achieving stability under heterogeneous channel conditions and robustness to heterogeneous data distributions. This paper analyzes the convergence and corroborates the superiority of Joint EQFL.

title: Independent PPO-based Robust and Scalable Three-dimensional Configuration for Green Massive MIMO Networks
authors: Qichen Wang, Shuai Zhang, Mustafa Ozger, Cicek Cavdar
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: In green massive MIMO networks, reducing power consumption (PC) while ensuring user quality of service (QoS) is critical for sustainable operation. To this end, we propose a robust and scalable reinforcement learning framework based on independent proximal policy optimization (IPPO), enabling intelligent base station (BS) control through a three-dimensional configuration of antenna activation, sleep mode transitions, and user offloading. Compared to a non-learning simple energy-saving policy, our proposed IPPO algorithm achieves approximately a 20.3% reduction in PC and a 49% improvement in energy efficiency (EE). In addition, it demonstrates significantly faster convergence and better scalability than multi-agent PPO (MAPPO), reducing convergence time by approximately 75% with 49 BSs and by around 90% with 81 BSs.

title: DiffPixelFormer: Differential Pixel-Aware Transformer for RGB-D Indoor Scene Segmentation
authors: Yan Gong, Jianli Lu, Yongsheng Gao, Jie Zhao, Xiaojuan Zhang, Susanto Rahardja
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Indoor semantic segmentation is fundamental to computer vision and robotics, supporting applications such as autonomous navigation, augmented reality, and smart environments. Although RGB-D fusion leverages complementary appearance and geometric cues, existing methods often depend on computationally intensive cross-attention mechanisms and insufficiently model intra- and inter-modal feature relationships, resulting in imprecise feature alignment and limited discriminative representation. To address these challenges, we propose DiffPixelFormer, a differential pixel-aware Transformer for RGB-D indoor scene segmentation that simultaneously enhances intra-modal representations and models inter-modal interactions. At its core, the Intra-Inter Modal Interaction Block (IIMIB) captures intra-modal long-range dependencies via self-attention and models inter-modal interactions with the Differential–Shared Inter-Modal (DSIM) module to disentangle modality-specific and shared cues, enabling fine-grained, pixel-level cross-modal alignment. Furthermore, a dynamic fusion strategy balances modality contributions and fully exploits RGB-D information according to scene characteristics. Extensive experiments on the SUN RGB-D and NYUDv2 benchmarks demonstrate that DiffPixelFormer-L achieves mIoU scores of 54.28% and 59.95%, outperforming DFormer-L by 1.78% and 2.75%, respectively. Moreover, its effectiveness is further validated on the large-scale ScanNetv2 dataset, indicating strong generalization capability. Code is available at https://github.com/gongyan1/DiffPixelFormer.

title: Adversarial Bandit Learning Assisted Online Optimization for Digital Twin Placement and Update in End-Edge-Cloud Collaboration
authors: Yuye Yang, Changyan Yi, Haifeng Zhu, Shimin Gong, Jiawen Kang, Dusit Niyato
abstract: Digital twin (DT) is envisioned not only to perform the high-fidelity virtual representation of its corresponding physical entity (PE), but also to serve as an active agent delivering diverse types of sophisticated services. This paper studies an end-edge-cloud collaborative DT placement and update framework. Specifically, we consider that DTs are dynamically placed across edge servers (ESs) via migration following their paired PEs' potential mobility, while being supported by real-time data fetched from the cloud center and user ends. On top of this, we emphasize a unique feature that DTs should also be continually updated capturing the uncertain evolutions for both personalized service ability improvement and versatile service ability maintenance, where the personalization is improved by utilizing the experiential knowledge from the cloud center and their corresponding PEs, and the versatility is maintained by integrating pre-stored profiles. To maximize the long-term system-wide average weighted quality-of-service (QoS) in handling all types of PEs' service requests under the stringent system cost constraint, we formulate an online problem to jointly optimize DT migrations, service priorities towards various request types, and all related DT updating strategies. To address underlying difficulties, we propose a novel adversarial bandit learning assisted online optimization approach, called ARBOK. We first leverage the Lyapunov decomposition method to transform the long-term problem into multiple instant ones, each of which is further decoupled into two correlated subproblems. For solving one subproblem with a bilinear structure, we develop a McCormick envelopes based algorithm (MO-EL). Besides, we design an extended adversarial combinatorial multi-armed bandit algorithm (AC-BL) to tackle the other subproblem, which constructs a super arm set to resolve the issue of excessively large decision space and employs a robust scheme to handle the inherent uncertainty and non-stationarity in each super arm's loss function. We integrate both algorithms seamlessly into ARBOK and alternately execute them till the convergence. Theoretical analysis and extensive simulations show the effectiveness of the introduced dynamic DT placement and continual update framework, demonstrating that ARBOK can converge to the asymptotic optimum within a polynomial-time complexity while outperforming counterparts.

title: Rethinking Cross-Layer Dependency in MPQ: Sparsification with PSD Framework
authors: Hyesung Lee, Minkyu Jung, Kyungrak Son
abstract: Mixed-precision quantization (MPQ) is a vital technique for deep learning model compression. In IQP-based MPQ, the sensitivity matrix capturing cross-layer dependencies is often not positive semi-definite (PSD), and the conventional eigenvalue-clipping correction introduces ordering distortion that can lead to suboptimal bit allocations. In this paper, we propose a novel MPQ framework using Shift-PSD, a structure-preserving PSD correction method combined with block-level sparsification. Our approach provably eliminates ordering distortion while preserving the exact optimal solutions, simultaneously achieving lightweight computation. Specifically, our two-stage framework first sparsifies the sensitivity matrix to remove noisy long-range interactions, and then enforces the PSD property via a spectral shift that adds only a constant offset over the feasible set. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves superior accuracy with lower computational cost (BitOps) in the highly compressed region.

title: Visual Privacy: The Impact of Privacy Labels on Privacy Behaviors Online
authors: Kimberly Garcia, Aurelia Tamò-Larrieux, Yannic Meier, Simon Mayer, Christoph Lutz, Clement Guitton, Leonardo Stern, Raffael Rot, Simeon Pilz, Stephan Mulders
abstract: None

title: Constraint-Scheduled Bayesian Optimization for Software-Hardware Co-Optimization on HDnn-PIM
authors: Chien-Yi Yang, Flavio Ponzina, Minxuan Zhou, Raid Ayoub, Pietro Mercati, Tajana Rosing
abstract: Real-world software–hardware co-design for AI accelerators must meet strict constraints on accuracy and PPA, making design space exploration both costly and inefficient. In this work, we define a metric-constrained multi-objective co-optimization problem and propose a constraint-scheduled Bayesian optimization framework, CSBO, that adapts constraint thresholds during the search. This scheduling balances exploration in early iterations with exploitation in later ones, improving both optimization efficiency and feasibility under metric constraints. Applied to HDnn-PIM, a state-of-the-art ML accelerator, our method increases feasible hypervolume and feasibility rate across datasets, while improving accuracy by 4.9%, energy by 287×, performance by 14×, and area by 2× compared to prior design through trade-offs.

title: EASNet: An Adaptive Edge-Aware 3D Medical Image Segmentation Network Based on the Swin Architecture
authors: Chengjie Chen, Jiang Wu, Liang Wang
abstract: To address the challenge of segmenting organs and tumors in medical images caused by low pixel contrast, this study proposes an Edge-Aware Self-Adaptive Network (EASNet), a 3D medical image segmentation network that integrates adaptive weight matching and attention mechanisms.The proposed method consists of three main components. First, the encoder combines a Swin Transformer with downsampling operations to achieve multi-scale feature extraction and global context modeling. Second, an AdaptiveWeight Matching (AWM) module dynamically adjusts the feature weights between the encoder and decoder based on channel characteristics, enabling efficient feature fusion. Third, the decoder incorporates a Hybrid Attention Convolution (HAC) module with both channel and spatial attention mechanisms to enhance the interaction between high-level and low-level features. Experimental results demonstrate that EASNet outperforms existing methods in terms of the Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC) on the BTCV and BraTS 2021 datasets, achieving a DSC of 88.65% on the Synapse dataset and a low 95th-percentile Hausdorff Distance (HD95) of 7.76. These results indicate that the proposed method exhibits superior performance in both multi-organ segmentation and brain tumor segmentation tasks.

title: Intrusion Detection System To Secure AI-powered E-Learning Platforms Against Various Cyberattacks
authors: Abdisamad Ayoubi, Loubna Laaouina, Adil Jeghal, Hamid Tairi
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: AI-powered E-learning represents a major step forward toward more effective and flexible education. Despite the challenges it poses, the opportunities it offers make it an essential choice for improving the educational process in the future. The integration of AI is mainly achieved through E-learning platforms, which are the key technological support. When powered by AI, these platforms become not only learning spaces, but also intelligent environments. However, this technological evolution introduces new vulnerabilities and attack surfaces. Cyberattacks via advanced attacks exploit these flaws to compromise the confidentiality, integrity, and reliability of the system, affecting the effectiveness and credibility of these platforms. It is therefore essential to identify these emerging risks and implement intelligent security mechanisms to protect digital learning platforms. This also requires constant attention and well-defined security measures to ensure an educational environment that is not only innovative but also safe, reliable, and secure.This article examines the main cyber risks faced by online learning platforms that integrate artificial intelligence. It highlights the value of integrating intrusion detection systems (IDS) based on deep learning techniques, particularly for identifying both traditional attacks and those specifically related to the use of AI in smart educational environments. The article also proposes a suitable architecture for this system, accompanied by countermeasures designed to enhance the security of AI-powered E-learning platforms.

title: Human Factors In Cyber Incident Response: Mitigating Biases, Enhancing Communication, And Optimizing Tool Usability
authors: Harchi Anas, Hicham Toumi, Mohamed Talea
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: This paper examines how important human characteristics affect the capability of teams managing cyber incidents. Three important components are emphasized: the impact of cognitive biases (CB) on decision-making, the interaction of communication in response teams, and the usefulness of security tools. We conduct a meticulous literature review alongside case study evaluations to pinpoint the difficulties in achieving effective incident response (IR). Our findings indicate that usual, technology-centered tactics often ignore the substantial ramifications of human mistakes and CB. We propose an integrated framework combining structured debiasing techniques, adaptive communication protocols, and cognitive load-optimized interfaces. The framework integrates with existing open-source platforms, providing practical solutions for organizations seeking to enhance IR capabilities through human-centered design. This paper presents a conceptual framework synthesized from extensive literature review and case analysis, establishing a foundation for future empirical validation in operational IR environments.

title: Architectural Analysis of a Lightweight Autonomous Vehicle Simulator: A Case Study of gym-donkeycar’s Modularity, Flexibility, and Adaptability for Aerial Robotics
authors: Leila Kelmoua, Zakaria Haja, Rahmoune Mohammed, Jamal Berrich, Toumi Bouchentouf
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: This paper provides an in-depth architectural assessment of the gym-donkeycar simulator, focusing on its modularity, flexibility, and potential for adaptation across different autonomous system applications. While the Python client—built on a decoupled client-server design and the OpenAI Gym interface—facilitates seamless integration of various algorithms, the Unity-based server relies on a monolithic, domain-specific physics engine restricted to a 4 -wheeled vehicle model, limiting broader applicability. A case study on unmanned aerial vehicle integration reveals fundamental gaps in dynamics, control structures, and sensor configurations. Based on these findings, the paper outlines targeted architectural improvements aimed at transforming the platform into a more general-purpose, multi-domain simulation tool, while reflecting on the inherent trade-offs between ease of use and generalizability in research simulators. This work also provides the first detailed architectural evaluation of gym-donkeycar and proposes a clear roadmap for adapting lightweight simulators to new robotic domains.

title: Disentangling EV Charging Behavior: An Integrated and Explainable Framework for Predicting Session Duration and Energy
authors: Radhika Bhati, Deepanshu Aggarwal, Meenakshi Malik, Ranbir Singh
abstract: The accurate prediction of electric vehicle (EV) charging behavior is necessary for grid stability but is undermined by population heterogeneity. In this paper, we introduce a unified and interpretable machine learning method that moves beyond traditional monolithic or compartmentalized modeling paradigms. We train a single gradient boosting model on vehicle metadata and new “user archetype” features learned through unsupervised clustering methods. With evaluation on a large real-world data set, our model demonstrates a significant improvement over state-of-the-art, with a Median Absolute Percentage Error (MedAPE) of 23.2% for charging duration and 24.1% for energy. Our main contribution, revealed through SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations), reveals a telling difference: charging duration prediction is inherently a behavioral task driven by user behavior and time aspects, whereas energy prediction is inherently a physics-based task controlled by battery capacity. This enlightening distinction allows for the design of more efficient smart charging schemes.

title: Prediction Model of Tobacco Purity Combining Hyperspectral Technology and Machine Learning
authors: Yonggang Liu, Ziyu Liu
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: The main purpose of this article is to develop an efficient prediction model of tobacco purity by combining hyperspectral imaging (HSI) technology and machine learning (ML). In this study, the detailed spectral information of tobacco samples was obtained by HSI technology, and the data quality was optimized through pretreatment and feature extraction. Then, the convolutional neural network (CNN) is used to deeply learn these data, automatically extract features and build a classification model. The results show that the accuracy, recall and F1 value of this model reach 95%, 93% and 93.1% respectively, which is significantly better than traditional algorithms such as support vector machine (SVM) and decision tree (DT). The response time of the system is only 5 seconds, which greatly improves the detection efficiency. In one month's actual production line test, 720 tobacco samples can be detected every hour, and the detection failure rate is as low as 0.5%. The research shows that the method based on the combination of HSI and CNN improves the speed and accuracy of tobacco purity detection and provides a new solution for the quality control of tobacco industry.

title: Research on Intelligent Modeling and Complex Relation Reasoning Algorithm of Large-scale Heterogeneous Data Based on Graph Neural Network
authors: Yushen Li, Jiawei Cui
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: How to effectively deal with multimodal, unstructured and dynamically evolving data has become a key challenge in the field of artificial intelligence. Traditional methods are difficult to capture the complex semantic association and dynamic dependency in data, while graph neural network (GNN) has made some progress through graph structure modeling, but it still has limitations in heterogeneous semantic fusion, dynamic relationship reasoning and computational efficiency. This study proposes the Dynamic Heterogeneous Graph Neural Network (DHGNN), which provides solutions to three core problems. Firstly, it dynamically learns the weights of different types of nodes/edges through Type-aware Attention Aggregation (TAA), and combines Graph Contrastive Learning (GCL) to solve the problem of heterogeneous semantic loss and enhance the model's robustness to noisy data; Secondly, design a Temporal Meta path Generator (TMPG) that utilizes reinforcement learning to automatically search for the optimal inference path, while introducing counterfactual data to enhance the model's ability to explain causal relationships; Thirdly, we propose Hierarchical Graph Sampling (HGS), which combines node level and subgraph level sampling to reduce redundant computation, and implements distributed training based on parameter servers to support real-time inference of billion level node graphs. The experimental results show that on ACM, Amazon and Alibaba-F data sets, DHGNN is significantly superior to the existing baseline model in terms of heterogeneous semantic fusion (Macro-F1 reaches 86.7, AUC reaches 92.4), dynamic reasoning (MRR reaches 0.89, search time is only 1.52s) and large-scale graph calculation efficiency (training time is shortened to 37 minutes, reasoning delay is 89ms). This research provides an efficient solution for intelligent modeling and complex relational reasoning of large-scale heterogeneous data.

title: Construction of an Intelligent UAV-Based Non-Contact Check the Electric Path Planning and Recognition Model for High-Voltage Transmission Environments
authors: Xiubiao Li, Xiaojia Chi, Gaohao Wen, Pai Liu, Qingfeng Han
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: The traditional manual check the electric mode has the problems of high-altitude operation risk, low efficiency and high cost, while non-contact check the electric can effectively improve the safety. In this paper, a non-contact check the electric module is designed, which uses capacitive coupling sensor to collect electric field data in real time, and integrates electric field sensor, IMU (Inertial Measurement Unit) and GPS data through Kalman filter to improve the measurement accuracy. A CNN-LSTM hybrid model is constructed, in which the electric field time series data and infrared images are input, and the charged probability is output. The loss function of the model adopts weighted cross entropy. In the aspect of path planning, the RRT* algorithm is improved, and the electromagnetic field safety cost function is introduced to ensure that the UAV keeps a safe distance from the live line, and the dynamic obstacle trajectory is predicted based on the speed obstacle method, and the local path is updated by combining the model predictive control. Through fuzzy logic controller, the comprehensive decision-making and control system takes the remaining power, electric field risk level and task urgency as input variables, and outputs the path weight adjustment coefficient to dynamically balance safety and efficiency. Simulation and experimental results show that the system has stable performance, high accuracy, low false alarm rate and short average response time under different test conditions. The improved RRT* algorithm is superior to the traditional A* algorithm in terms of path length, time-consuming planning, safety and dynamic obstacle avoidance success rate, and has good anti-electromagnetic interference performance, which provides technical support for the intelligent application of UAV in the power industry and promotes the development of power grid transformation to digital intelligence.

title: Real-Time Offline Edge AI Framework for Sensor Integrated Precision Agriculture
authors: T G Manjunath, Sukruth Gowda M A, Kumaresh Sheelavant, Harika Harika, Itikala Sriram, Monika M
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Real-time agricultural decision-making in small-scale and rural farming environments remains challenging due to unreliable connectivity, fragmented toolchains, and the absence of localized advisory systems. This paper presents a real-time offline edge AI framework for sensor-integrated precision agriculture, in which crop classification and fertilizer recommendation are performed entirely on-device without reliance on cloud infrastructure. The proposed system integrates an Arduino-based soil sensing unit capable of measuring Nitrogen (N), Phosphorus (P), Potassium (K), soil pH, temperature, humidity, and moisture with a lightweight edge-based machine learning pipeline. A supervised multi-class crop prediction model trained and evaluated on a curated Indian agro-climatic dataset consisting of 52,001 records across 13 crop classes achieves an accuracy of 96.28%, with macro-averaged precision, recall, and F1-score of 0.96. Unlike prior approaches that address crop estimate or fertilizer recommendation in isolation, the proposed framework unifies crop classification, dataset-driven fertilizer advisory, and rule-based disease risk alerts within a single fully offline system. Predictions are logged locally in a lightweight JSON store and visualized using pre-downloaded geographic boundaries, enabling effective real-time recommendations in low-resource and connectivity-constrained deployment settings.

title: DeepGynScan: AI-Powered Cervical Lesion Detection from Colposcopy Images for Early Cervical Cancer Screening
authors: Krishna Kumar R, Pratheebha G, Manasavarshini G D, Nivas S, Sathya D, Mohamme Rahamadullah
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Cervical cancer is one of the leading causes of cancer-related deaths among women globally and is highly prevalent in areas where there is limited access to specialist care and diagnostic infrastructure. Current screening methods like Pap smear cytology and colposcopy are highly subjective and rely on expert interpretation, which can introduce variability and delay in diagnosis. This work discusses DeepGynScan, an AI-driven framework using the EfficientNetB0 architecture for automated classification of cervical lesions into normal, low-grade, and high-grade categories. The system was trained on the publicly available SIPaKMeD cytology dataset and obtained an accuracy of 93% with a precision, recall, and F1-score of 0.93, 0.94, and 0.93, respectively. A web-based diagnostic platform integrated using FastAPI and React enables real-time image upload, inference, and automatic generation of a clinical report. Although the model is currently trained on cytology images different from the macroscopic colposcopy views, the proposed work mainly stands at the feasibility-stage investigation to showcase that lightweight deep-learning systems are capable of performing assessments of cervical lesions. Future work will focus on training and validation with large-scale annotated colposcopy datasets to reduce domain shift and further support clinical deployment.

title: Efficient Reinforcement Corrosion Detection via Lightweight YOLO with Dual-Branch Cross-Scale Feature Modulation
authors: Xiao Xiong, Yuanlong Yang, Wen Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive study on automated corrosion detection in reinforced concrete structures using deep learning techniques. A two-stage training strategy based on YOLO11n architecture is proposed to effectively identify corrosion damage through computer vision. The CONCORNET2023 dataset, comprising 790 images of corroded concrete structures, was utilized for training and validation. Stage 1 employed default hyperparameters for initial model convergence, while Stage 2 incorporated fine-tuned parameters to address overfitting and enhance detection accuracy. Experimental results demonstrate that the two-stage approach achieves improved precision (51.83%) and maintains consistent performance across validation sets. The proposed methodology offers a robust, non-destructive solution for infrastructure maintenance and safety assessment, significantly reducing human inspection costs and time requirements.

title: Research on Image Recognition and Data Optimization of Intelligent Management System of Power Warehouse Driven by Meta-Universe
authors: Yu Zhang, Qiang Guo, Rui Ding, Lei Yu, Xiaolei Tian
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: Aiming at the pain points in power equipment warehouse management, such as fragile labels, low efficiency of manual inspection and difficulty in monitoring extreme environment, this paper proposes a meta-universe-driven intelligent management system for power warehouses, focusing on overcoming two technical bottlenecks: image recognition and dynamic data optimization under complex illumination. The system adopts "five-layer collaboration" architecture, takes digital twinning as the core, and integrates 5G+TSN network, edge computing and Unity three-dimensional modeling to realize real-time mapping and closed-loop regulation between physical warehouse and virtual space. On the level of image recognition, an improved YOLOv7-CBAM model is proposed, and Retinex adaptive illumination compensation and BiFPN multi-scale feature fusion are introduced. The average recognition accuracy is 93.4% in rain and fog, strong reflection and low illumination scenes, which is significantly better than the baseline of Faster R-CNN and YOLOv7. On the data optimization level, GCN-GRU spatio-temporal joint inventory forecasting model and GAN-Q-learning fusion path planning strategy are constructed, so that the MAPE of transformer inventory forecasting in the next seven days is controlled within 5%, and the average time consumption and energy consumption of AGV emergency tasks are reduced to 29.1 min and 12.6 kWh respectively, and the task achievement rate is improved to 97%. The experiment is based on the data of 12 000 m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> real power warehouse. The coincidence degree between the digital twin preview trajectory and the actual operation is 98.2%, and the potential collision risk of the whole system can be reduced by 23%. The research results provide a reproducible technical path for the metauniverse landing of power industry.

title: SLAM Technology Based on Multi-View 3D Reconstruction
authors: Penghui Wang, Yu Bai
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) has been widely applied in unmanned aerial vehicles and robotic systems. However, due to the limited computational capability of most edge devices, achieving efficient and accurate mapping in a lightweight manner remains a major challenge. To address this issue, this paper proposes a SLAM system named SLAM_Fusion, which is built upon multi-view 3D reconstruction. The system performs real-time and accurate dense 3D reconstruction using only monocular RGB images. By employing a bottleneck-architecture-based fusion mechanism and an enhancement module that leverages fused information to refine the original views, SLAM_Fusion achieves lightweight yet accurate local mapping and robust global optimization. Experiments conducted on multiple datasets demonstrate that SLAM_Fusion attains high reconstruction accuracy and completeness while maintaining real-time performance.

title: A Lightweight Attention Dual-Branch Network for Low-Elevation DOA Estimation
authors: Siqi Ni, Yanping Liao, Hang Jiang, Mingfei Zhao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: To address the challenges of significant multipath interference, limited elevation estimation accuracy, and inadequate real-time performance in low-elevation direction-ofarrival (DOA) estimation, this paper presents a lightweight attention-based dual-branch network. The proposed method employs a dual-branch architecture that independently optimizes azimuth and elevation estimation, while a lightweight hybrid attention module adaptively enhances angle-sensitive features. Furthermore, A two-stage dynamic training strategy is introduced, which incorporates an adaptive learning-rate decay mechanism to balance training efficiency and model performance, and erroraware loss-weighting to balance azimuth-elevation joint learning. Compared with traditional two-dimensional DOA estimation algorithms, the proposed method achieves higher accuracy in elevation-angle estimation and faster inference speed.

title: Enhanced Deep Learning Framework for Diagnosing Rare Genetic and Dermatological Disorders using Fine-Tuned EfficientNetB1
authors: Ravada Sony, Chiranjeevi Manike
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Estimating the impact of rare skin diseases is challenging because at most cases them look may similar to existing ones. This work introduces a boosted diagnostic pipeline built on the DermNet dataset, which covers 23 skin-disease categories. Have fine-tuned an EfficientNetB1 core, added Batch Normalization to improve training stability, and Swish activation to extract richer features. Custom enhancement schemes that affect brightness and color channels prepare models for different images in the real world. The system offers a high level of performance, with accuracy and recall over 92%. Some classes, such as nail-fungus, reach better F1 scores, and detection of critical diseases such as measles remains highly sensitive. The proposed methodology provides a robust and scalable tool for reducing dermatology diagnostic errors.

title: Design, Simulation, and Performance Evaluation of a D77 DC Traction Motor Converted to AC Operation
authors: Morteza Hemmati, Sara Javadianfar, Hossein Jamshidpour
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: Nearly 45% of the Iranian railway traction fleet still operates with conventional D77 DC motors, which relay on commutators and carbon brushes. These units, although once reliable, now frequently fail, require intensive maintenance, and exhibit limited efficiency compared to modern systems. This study presents a practical design and analysis procedure for converting the D77 DC traction motor into an equivalent AC motor while preserving the original housing and mechanical interface. Based on the D77's dimensions and electromagnetic limits, a new three-phase, four-pole, squirrel-cage induction motor, named A77, was developed. The redesign encompassed key aspects, including stator slot geometry, winding layout, and rotor bar material selection. Detailed electromagnetic and thermal analyses were conducted using ANSYS Motor-CAD to evaluate the proposed configuration. Simulation results indicated an efficiency of approximately 95% and a power factor of 0.78. In addition to improved thermal management, the new motor eliminates brush-related failures and significantly reduces maintenance requirements. The results confirm that upgrading existing DC motors to AC operation is a technically and economically viable solution for extending the service life of Iran's aging traction fleet. To the best of our knowledge, this work provides the first documented case of a D77-to-AC conversion and offers a framework that can be adapted to similar retrofit projects in the railway industry.

title: Research on Bipolar Short-Circuit Fault Current Clearing Method for T-type DC Transformers
authors: Zhang Zhibo, Liu Yali, Lin Zhiguang, Deng Weihua, Li Yuanzhen, Cheng Tingting
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy (ICSGE)
abstract: The application of T-type DC transformers is hindered by the challenge of clearing bipolar short-circuit faults, as established method of configuring DC breakers often lead to increase the volume and cost of the DC transformer. To address this limitation, this paper proposes a bipolar short-circuit fault current clearing method for the T-type DC transformer. It is demonstrated that configuring a certain number of full-bridge submodules (FBSMs) in the high-voltage arm can not only absorbs the fault freewheeling current in the arms after blocking but also suppresses the discharge current fed into the low-voltage port of the DC transformer. Firstly, the fault equivalent circuit and fault development mechanism under bipolar short-circuit faults are analyzed. Secondly, the fault equivalent model and the fault current clearing process following blocking are studied. Thirdly, the design principles for designing the number of full-bridge submodules in the high-voltage arm are proposed. Finally, the proposed fault current clearing method is verified by offline simulations in PSCAD/EMTDC. The offline EMT simulation results show that the fault current is completely cleared within approximately 2 ms, faster than the opening time of DC breakers, while maintaining safe operating voltages across submodule capacitors.

title: Higher Order Sliding Robust Control Design for Single-Stage Plugin Electric Vehicle
authors: Mohammad Asif Ali, Aman Keshri, Dogga Raveendhra, Phaneendra Babu Bobba, BL Narasimha Raju
abstract: This manuscript contains robust control technique for controlling the gate signal of single-stage on-board charger. In conventional controller PID is used for generating the control pulse. As the power converter is highly non-linear in nature therefore the linear controller which operates on and around equilibrium point is prone to disturbance. Non-linear controller would be better option for controlling the dynamics of power converter. Sliding mode control technique is recommended for robust control, however it introduces chattering phenomenon. For tackling the issue, this paper uses Super twisting sliding mode control based on Higher order sliding algorithm. Two sliding surface is selected for PFC correction and output voltage control. Lyapunov's stability theory has been utilized to develop the reaching control law for the power converter.

title: XGBoost for Type 2 Diabetes Prediction with Precision-Recall Thresholding
authors: Shuvra Baral, Divya Bhattarai, Ashim Khadka
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Science and Communications Technologies (ICISCT)
abstract: Type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM) is a global health crisis, affecting 537 million adults in 2021 and projected to reach 783 million by 2045, with annual costs of USD 966 billion. Early detection is essential to prevent complications like kidney disease and cardiovascular issues. However, XGBoost exhibits low recall, approximately 0.58 on the PIMA Indian Diabetes Dataset, due to default threshold optimization, biologically implausible zeros (i.e., 48.7 percentage in Insulin), outliers, and 34.9 percentage diabetic class imbalance. This paper addresses these challenges by developing an XGBoost-based model with KNN imputation $(k=5)$ for zeros, log transformation for skewness, interquartile range (IQR) capping to manage outliers, and precision-recall thresholding with a target of 0.7 to boost recall while maximizing precision. Hyperparameter tuning via GridSearchCV optimizes the model, guided by logistic loss. Results show significant recall improvement to 0.85, with an F2 score of 0.774 and G-mean of 0.744, surpassing baseline XGBoost and Random Forest. The proposed model advances T2DM prediction by prioritizing early detection, offering clinical relevance through interpretable and noise-resistant modeling.

title: High-Resolution Processing and Feature Extraction of Logging Curves Based on Wavelet Transform and Principal Component Analysis
authors: Jinghao Hu
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: With the development of oil and gas exploration towards refinement, deeper formations, and unconventional fields, higher requirements are placed on the resolution of logging data and the ability to mine information. Traditional single signal processing or dimensionality reduction methods have limitations in improving the resolution and feature recognition of logging curves, making it difficult to effectively balance signal detail enhancement and redundancy removal. To address this, this paper proposes a high-resolution processing and feature extraction algorithm for logging curves, WDW-PCA, based on wavelet transform and principal component analysis, aiming to achieve high-resolution processing of logging curves and effective extraction of multi-scale features. This paper first constructs a collaborative processing framework of frequency division-reconstruction-fusion. Utilizing the natural advantage of multi-resolution analysis of wavelet transform, the original logging signal is adaptively decomposed into different frequency bands, and threshold optimization and enhancement processing are performed on sub-bands containing high-frequency details and noise to improve performance. Secondly, this paper introduces dynamic principal component analysis to purify the features of the reconstructed multi-scale signals. Unlike conventional PCA, this algorithm dynamically determines the weights of principal components based on the energy contribution of each wavelet decomposition scale, thus maximizing the retention of essential features revealed by high-resolution processing that are sensitive to thin layers and complex reservoir spaces while reducing dimensionality, effectively suppressing information redundancy and noise interference. Validation with real data shows that this fusion algorithm significantly enhances the ability of logging curves to identify thin interlayers, achieving the goal of high-resolution processing. This study provides a new and effective technical approach for using conventional logging data to finely characterize reservoir heterogeneity.

title: A Review of Medium Access Control Protocols for Wireless Body Area Networks
authors: Haoru Su, Zichen Miao, Yanglong Sun, Huijie Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: As one of the key technologies in the Internet of Things (IoT), Wireless Body Area Networks (WBANs) face critical Medium Access Control (MAC) protocol design challenges like high energy efficiency, low latency, and high reliability. This paper summarizes recent advancements of WBAN MAC protocols, focusing on classifying them by two mainstream IEEE standards—IEEE 802.15.4 and IEEE 802.15.6—and their core optimizations: IEEE 802.15.4 protocols are optimized via parameter tuning, cross-layer design, and superframe enhancement; IEEE 802.15.4 protocols via parameter adaptability, cross-layer synergy, priority scheduling, and superframe improvement. It also identifies major challenges (e.g., energy-real-time trade-off, insufficient adaptability, theory-practice gap) and prospects future trends, providing a concise reference for WBAN MAC protocol design.

title: Federated Explainable Transformer for Early Cancer Diagnosis using MRI Data
authors: Shanmugapriya R, Sruthi M, K.Jasmine Mystica
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The detection of prostate cancer using MRI is challenged by the sparsity of voxel-level annotations and the heterogeneity in the appearance of the tumor. Classic fully supervised deep learning models are required to work with large annotated datasets, which are often infeasible in the clinical setup. A mixed supervision paradigm is introduced that leverages the combination of weakly labeled (patient-level) and strongly labeled (voxel-level) histopathology datasets for the purpose of enhanced prostate cancer classification using MRI. A Hybrid CNN-Transformer model is utilized where a ResNet-50 backbone extracts local features and a Transformer module infers long-range contextual relations between regions. A branch of the model learns from a weak label using multi-instance learning while the second learns from strong histopathology labels for accurate feature selection. The novelty lies in the combination of multi-scale supervision and hybrid CNN-Transformer modeling that allows for efficient localization and classification of the tumor even when voxel-level labels are scarce. Experimental assessments using multi-center datasets show superior performance when contrasted with classic fully supervised and weakly supervised techniques that highlight its clinical applicability.

title: ST-MSHT-Net: A Soft-Thresholding Multi-Stream Hybrid Temporal Network for Automatic Modulation Recognition
authors: Zhaoqi Cai, Yuan’An Liu, Dongming Yuan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: Automatic Modulation Recognition (AMR) is a key technology in cognitive radio and spectrum monitoring systems operating in complex electromagnetic environments. Although deep learning has achieved promising results, extracting discriminative features from high-dimensional signals and modeling long-term temporal dependencies under complex channel conditions remain challenging. To address these issues, this paper proposes a Soft-Thresholding Multi-Stream Hybrid Temporal Network (ST-MSHT-Net). The proposed framework adopts a six-stream multi-representation input mechanism, incorporating In-Phase/Quadrature (I/Q), Amplitude/Phase (A/P), and their two-dimensional coupled representations to enhance feature completeness. An adaptive soft-thresholding module with a learnable parameter is introduced to perform early-stage feature refinement by enforcing end-to-end sparsity constraints. Furthermore, a hybrid temporal architecture composed of LSTM-MHA-LSTM is designed to jointly capture local temporal dynamics and global contextual dependencies. Experiments conducted on the RadioML2016.10a benchmark dataset demonstrate that ST-MSHT-Net achieves a peak recognition accuracy of $\mathbf{9 3. 0 4 \%}$. Across the full SNR range from $-\mathbf{2 0 ~ d B}$ to 18 dB and the high SNR regime from 0 dB to 18 dB, the proposed model attains average accuracies of 62.6% and 91.9%, respectively, outperforming representative baseline methods.

title: Construction and Optimization of Image Recognition Fusion Model for Multi-Device Collaboration
authors: Yuqing Xie, Xiaoke Xu, Tao Zhang, Wenxue Cao
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: With the rapid development of edge computing and smart terminals, the image recognition task is gradually evolving from single-device processing to multi-device collaboration in order to adapt to the multiple requirements of real-time, accuracy and energy efficiency in complex environments. In this paper, we propose a fusion model construction and optimization method for multi-device collaboration scenarios, aiming to make full use of the collaborative capability among heterogeneous devices from multiple sources to achieve efficient division of labor and intelligent fusion of image recognition tasks. An image recognition fusion model based on collaborative architecture is constructed to support cross-device information integration at the feature and decision layers; secondly, the alignment and preprocessing mechanisms for multi-source image data are designed to improve the consistency and robustness of the fusion input; finally, comprehensive strategies such as model lightweighting, heterogeneous task scheduling, and energy-efficiency optimization are proposed to improve the overall system performance in response to resource constraints and real-time requirements. Experimental results show that the proposed method realizes the balance of recognition accuracy, inference speed and system energy consumption in multiple real-world scenarios, which verifies the potential and practical value of the fusion model in multi-device collaboration.

title: Semantic Resume Screening using Transformer Networks and Learning-to-Rank Models
authors: Aravinda Reddy Putluru, Priyanshu Arora, Ashokkumar C, Jothi Kumar C
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The exponential growth of digital recruitment platforms has resulted in organizations receiving a large volume of resumes for every job opening, making manual screening inefficient, inconsistent, and prone to bias. To address these challenges, this work presents the implementation of a transformer-based deep learning framework for automated resume shortlisting and ranking. The proposed system leverages advanced Natural Language Processing and Learning-to-Rank techniques to semantically match candidate resumes with job requirements and automatically shortlist the most relevant candidates. The framework integrates BERT-based Named Entity Recognition for robust resume parsing and structured information extraction, including skills, job roles, experience, and education. Sentence-BERT embeddings are employed to perform semantic skill and education matching, enabling context-aware similarity computation beyond traditional keyword-based approaches. A Transformer Cross-Encoder is used to evaluate role relevance by capturing fine-grained semantic interactions between candidate experience and target job roles. Education is enforced as a mandatory constraint, ensuring compliance with organizational hiring criteria. For final ranking, a RankNet-based Learning-to-Rank model is implemented to optimally order candidates based on skill relevance, experience, role alignment, and education suitability. The system uses a fixed, transparent weighting scheme to maintain interpretability and auditability. Performance evaluation using standard ranking metrics such as Precision@5, Recall@5, Mean Reciprocal Rank, and NDCG demonstrates that the proposed approach effectively prioritizes relevant candidates and aligns closely with human recruiter judgments. The implemented system significantly reduces recruitment screening time while improving ranking accuracy, fairness, and explainability, making it suitable for real-world deployment in intelligent recruitment systems.

title: MetaWeave: Interpretable Fusion of Text and Fraud Detection for E-commerce Review Systems
authors: N. Noor Alleema, Kandi Syam Vishnu Vardhan Reddy, Maramreddy Lohith Sai Reddy, S. Jagadeesh
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Online shopping is increasing, but many online reviews are fake, which misleads customers and damages businesses’ credibility. In this work, MetaWeave is described as the framework that has been developed for the more reliable detection of fake reviews. Instead of looking at text alone, as many older systems do, MetaWeave examines four types of clues: how the review is written, the time at which it was posted, how the reviewer and product are connected in the network, and also the behavior patterns of the reviewer. When these pieces are combined, the system gets a clearer picture whether a review is real or not. MetaWeave was tested on the OSF Fake Reviews dataset, containing 40,432 reviews across ten product groups. On the test split, MetaWeave reaches 95.0 percent ROC-AUC, an F1-score of 88.17 percent, and an accuracy of 88.20 percent. To avoid data leakage and to reflect real deployment conditions, a user-level data partitioning strategy is also introduced that ensures reviews from the same user do not appear in both training and testing sets. An interpretability study with SHAP and LIME is also provided to identify which linguistic, structural, and behavioral features influence the model’s predictions.

title: Design of Anomaly Attribution Algorithms for Network Anomalies Under Multi-source Heterogeneous Data Fusion
authors: Baosheng Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: In order to improve the attribution ability of network anomaly in heterogeneous environment, we build a framework of feature representation and chain reasoning based on time alignment, semantic fusion, and heterogeneous graph modeling. The proposed approach achieves a unified representation of cross-source events by a multi-level coding structure, whereas attention mechanisms enhance the response at critical nodes. In the process of attribution, a probabilistic path inference and a heuristic pruning strategy are used to construct causal chains. The results show that the framework has a consistent advantage in Precision, F1-SCORE, and Path Accuracy, while maintaining low inference latency in high-load situations. The results show that the framework is robust and robust in detecting anomalies.

title: Multi-Objective Design Optimization of a Flux-Switching Permanent Magnet Motor: A Comparative Study of Metaheuristic Algorithms
authors: Tohid Sharifi, Ali Jamali-Fard, Hossein Azizi Moghaddam
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: The inevitable and intrinsic contradiction between the most required performance criteria of electric motors, such as torque performance, manufacturing/material cost, reliability, compactness, and mechanical integrity has guided the researchers to exploit the intelligent multi-objective algorithms for the design optimization problems, rather than the classical single-objective approaches. In this study, the performance criteria of a flux-switching permanent magnet motor is studied as a benchmark case to compare the quantity and quality of the results, proposed by the most popular multi-objective metaheuristic algorithms, including NSGA-II, MOPSO, SPEA-II, and MOGWO. In the comparative study phase, the numerical finite element analysis (FEA) is utilized in interface with the optimization algorithms. The non-dominated design solutions, already available in the form of Pareto fronts, are then evaluated based on a decision making strategy and a single solution is extracted from the entire set of solutions proposed by all algorithms. The finally selected optimal design is manufactured and tested to validated the results of the FEA as the last step of this study.

title: A Heterogeneous Easy Ensemble with Soft Voting for Robust Sleep Disorder Classification
authors: Kishor Kumar Yadav, Shuvra Baral, Ashim Khadka
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Science and Communications Technologies (ICISCT)
abstract: Sleep disorders significantly impair cognitive function, emotional stability, and overall health. Sleep disorder classification remains challenging due to class imbalance and nonlinear data patterns. This paper presents a heterogeneous Easy Ensemble framework that integrates Support Vector Machine (SVM), Multilayer Perceptron (MLP), Decision Tree (DT), and K-Nearest Neighbor (KNN) to enhance sleep disorder detection. A lifestyle and physiological dataset of 374 subjects is analyzed, with highly correlated features removed using Pearson correlation analysis to reduce multicollinearity. Three balanced bootstrap subsets are generated through random undersampling of the majority class. Individual classifiers are trained on each subset, and their probability outputs are aggregated via soft voting. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed ensemble consistently outperforms state-of-the-art homogeneous ensembles such as XGBoost, CatBoost, and Random Forest. On the most balanced subset, the model achieved recall of 0.948, specificity of 0.978, G-Mean of 0.967, and accuracy of 0.95, surpassing homogeneous ensembles in both sensitivity and selectivity. Clinical interpretation indicates that the proposed model misses only about 5 percent of disorder cases and wrongly flags about 2 percent of healthy individuals, compared to 6 to 14 percent and 5 percent respectively for tree based methods. The approach enhances prediction stability and interpretability, offering a reliable solution for clinical sleep disorder screening.

title: Research on Fault Diagnosis and Predictive Maintenance Algorithm of Complex Industrial System Based on Graph Neural Network
authors: Kongduo Xing, Yetong Wang, Haifeng Lu, Dawei Yun
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: Traditional maintenance mode can't meet the demand, but graph neural network (GNN) has advantages in processing graph structure data, which provides the possibility for solving related problems. This paper proposes a framework based on dynamic GNN to realize fault diagnosis and predictive maintenance of complex industrial systems. Its main contributions are as follows: firstly, the self-adaptive graph topology is constructed by combining the physical connection of devices and the correlation analysis driven by data to solve the contradiction between static graph assumption and system dynamics; The second is to design an improved TGAT (Temporary Graph Attention Network) module that integrates time sequence characteristics and topological relations to enhance the ability to identify equipment coupling faults; Thirdly, a multi-task learning framework is established, and the fault classification and residual life prediction (RLP) are jointly optimized, and the dynamic weight allocation mechanism is adopted to improve the diagnosis accuracy in small sample fault scenarios. In terms of research methods, firstly, the graph structure that changes with time is defined, and the construction of dynamic edge set is emphasized. The weights of edges are combined with physical connection strength and dynamic correlation coefficient, and learning fusion weights are introduced. Secondly, an improved TGAT module is designed, which uses Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) coding time sequence characteristics, introduces dynamic attention mechanism to update node representation, and adopts robustness enhancement strategy. Finally, the multi-task learning framework is constructed, and the dynamic weighted loss function and the dynamic weighting mechanism of fault sample proportion perception are designed. Experiments were carried out on TE data set and compressor set data set of chemical plant. The results show that the proposed method is superior to many baseline models in fault classification and RLP. The ablation experiments verify the effectiveness of each module, and the model has good robustness to noise, so it is feasible and reliable to make accurate life prediction in actual industrial scenes.

title: Pattern Recognition of Lower Limb EMG Signals Based on Hyperparameter - Optimized Random Forest
authors: Junyao Wang, Xinqiang Zhu
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: To address the limitation of traditional random forests that rely on universal default parameters and cannot adapt to the characteristics of EMG (Electromyography) signals, resulting in low classification accuracy. This paper proposed a HPO-RF (Hyperparameter Optimized Random Forest) algorithm. The algorithm core is a data-driven hyperparameter optimization strategy designed to identify an optimal set of configurations highly tailored to the specific task, thereby significantly enhancing the model's discriminative power. To validate the algorithm's efficacy, a dataset comprising 7 distinct lower limb movements was established, and utilize the proposed HPO-RF algorithm for pattern recognition. The results demonstrate that the proposed HPO-RF algorithm achieved an average recognition accuracy of 95.97 % across the 7 movements. Its performance was significantly superior to the conventional RF with default parameters, as well as other classical machine learning and deep learning algorithms. This study provides a valuable and robust methodology for EMG-based motion classification.

title: Molecular simulation calculation of electrical properties of transformer insulation materials and expansion of cross-scale model application
authors: Wenlong Xu, Qiulin Chen, Ran Zhuo, Sicheng Zhao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: Oil-immersed power transformers are the core equipment of the power system. The performance of the oil-paper insulation system directly affects its operating status. As the solid insulation material in the insulation system, the types of insulation paper vary. The cross-scale model of dielectric properties based on dielectric theory and microstructural characteristics of insulating paper has been successfully applied to the rapid calculation of the dielectric constant of kraft paper. To address the issue of the applicability of the cross-scale mapping model, this study studied the dielectric constants of different types of insulating paper based on the cross-scale model. First, the microstructural characteristics of different types of insulating paper are observed through scanning electron microscopy. Then, the structures of different types of insulating paper are established through molecular simulation and their dielectric constants are solved. Finally, the dielectric constants of different types of insulating papers are obtained through the established cross-scale model and compared with experimental test values. The results show that the cross-scale model based on dielectric theory can be applied to the calculation of dielectric constants of different types of insulating paper.

title: Analyzing the Relationship Between Academic Performance and Enrollment Patterns Using Machine Learning
authors: Marjon D. Senarlo, James Cloyd M. Bustillo, Jeanie R. Delos Arcos
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: This study analyzes the relationship between academic performance and student enrollment patterns at Christ the King College de Maranding (CKCM) using machine learning techniques to develop a predictive model for identifying at-risk students. A dataset comprising 2,111 student records from the 2024–2025 academic year was utilized, where student enrollment status was represented through an "eligibility" classification based on three academic criteria: a General Weighted Average (GWA) of ≤ 2.5, zero failed subjects, and a minimum enrollment of 12 academic units. Four machine learning classifiers Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, Random Forest, and Gradient Boosting were trained and evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and cross-validation. Results revealed that both the Decision Tree and Gradient Boosting models achieved perfect performance, obtaining scores of 1.000 across all evaluation metrics in both test and validation phases. Correlation analysis further identified GWA and the total number of failed subjects as the most influential predictors of student eligibility. The findings demonstrate the effectiveness of machine learning in modeling enrollment-related outcomes and provide CKCM with a practical decision-support tool for early detection of at-risk students, enabling timely interventions, improved retention strategies, and informed institutional planning.

title: An Auction-based Matching Game for Cognitive Radio Heterogeneous Relay Networks
authors: Mennatallah A. Rostom, Ahmed H. Abd El-Malek, Mohammed Abo-Zahhad, Maha Elsabrouty
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: The increasing demand for higher data rates requires better utilization of the limited wireless resources, especially, the frequency spectrum. Cognitive radio technology aims to efficiently reuse the available licensed spectrum by allowing secondary users to use the spectrum opportunistically. Moreover, relays help in enhancing the coverage and quality-of-service of the users in the network. In this work, we consider a cognitive relaying framework for cognitive small cells. Cognitive relays decide the best of two strategies. The first is to relay the primary data and use the channel for their communication for a portion of the time frame. The second strategy is to borrow the primary channels for the complete time frame and in return compensate the primary base stations (PBSs). Meanwhile, the proposed scenario allows the PBSs to limit the secondary base stations (SBSs) using its bands to transmission powers that will not incur excessive interference on the primary communications. We introduce an auction-based matching game for a cognitive radio heterogeneous network to jointly allocate primary channels to the secondary users, and relay the primary communication through SBSs. Simulation results confirm the gain of the relaying for both, improving the rate of the primary user as well as satisfying the performance of the secondary users.

title: Identification and Early Warning Technology for Price Fluctuation Risk in the Electricity Spot Market
authors: Hanze Zhou, Kun Wang, Xiejun Du, Yuan Zhang, Hao Sun, Gang Luo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy (ICSGE)
abstract: With the rapid integration of renewable energy and the diversification of participants in the electricity spot market, price fluctuations have become increasingly frequent and uncertain, posing new challenges to market stability and operational decision-making. To address these issues, this paper proposes a risk identification and early warning framework for abnormal electricity price behavior. First, an evaluation index system is constructed based on the underlying market mechanism and influencing factors, covering multiple dimensions such as price dynamics, participant behavior, and system operating conditions. Second, data dimensionality reduction techniques, such as principal component analysis (PCA), are combined with two neural network-based nonlinear early warning models to enhance the model’s ability to detect and predict abnormal price fluctuations. Finally, using actual transaction data from a provincial electricity spot market, the proposed method is validated. The results demonstrate that the framework maintains high computational efficiency while accurately identifying abnormal volatility and issuing early warning signals in advance. The research provides technical support for risk prevention and control in complex market environments.

title: Partial offloading algorithm for edge computing based on MAPPO
authors: Wenhao Yu, Haofeng Wang, Manfei Zhao, Ning Zhang, Tong Gao, Zongyu Wang, Qiuyi Qu, Yanxiang Ji
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: As a distributed computing paradigm close to terminal devices, mobile edge computing provides offloading services for computation-intensive applications by deploying computing resources at the edge nodes of the network, which effectively solves the core bottleneck of limited computing power and low local processing efficiency of terminal devices. However, the resource capacity of edge nodes has a physical upper limit, and the wireless channel is affected by path loss and other dynamic time-varying characteristics. Traditional offloading strategies often ignore task heterogeneity and load change trends, resulting in problems such as unbalanced system load distribution and high-priority tasks timeout. To solve the above problems, this paper proposes a partial offloading algorithm combining the Analytic Hierarchy process, long short-term memory network, and multi-agent proximal policy optimization. Simulation results show that compared with the baseline scheme, the proposed algorithm significantly improves the comprehensive service performance and resource utilization efficiency of the MEC system.

title: A Systematic Review of YOLO Architectures for Real-Time Object Detection
authors: Unnati Gayaki, Lakshmi Madireedy
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The You Only Look Once (YOLO) framework has redefined real-time object detection by introducing a single-stage architecture that balances exceptional speed with competitive accuracy, establishing it as a pivotal technology in computer vision. This systematic review traces the evolution of YOLO architectures from YOLOv1 to YOLOv11, highlighting key innovations such as anchor boxes, multi-scale predictions, and Transformer integration, which have driven performance improvements, achieving mean Average Precision (mAP) up to 59% on COCO and speeds up to 440 frames per second (FPS) for lightweight variants. YOLO's applications span autonomous vehicles, healthcare, industrial automation, surveillance, agriculture, and sports analytics. This review examines architectural advancements, performance metrics, challenges such as small object detection and computational constraints, and future directions toward adaptive and ethical AI, providing a comprehensive foundation for advancing real-time object detection.

title: The Transformer as a Parametric Filter for Sequence MIMO Equalization
authors: Kai Yu, Yulan Gao, Yijing Lin, Zhonghao Lyu, Jiwei Zhao, Ming Xiao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: In-context learning (ICL) has emerged as a promising paradigm for MIMO receiver design, enabling rapid adaptation to dynamic wireless environments via a single forward pass without the need for explicit channel state information estimation. However, the deployment of standard Transformerbased ICL receivers is constrained by the quadratic computational complexity of the softmax attention mechanism. This paper develops a computationally efficient ICL receiver based on linear attention and establishes an explicit analytical mapping between a single-layer linear attention and a context-dependent parametric filter. We derive a closed-form expression showing that the receiver performs an implicit channel estimation step through an empirical cross-correlation matrix computed from the pilot context, followed by a learned preconditioning applied to the data symbols. The proposed architecture achieves a persymbol inference complexity of $\mathcal{O}\left(d^{2}\right)$ after an initial $\mathcal{O}\left(N_{p} d^{2}\right)$ pilot-state construction, thereby eliminating the quadratic dependence on the sequence length L present in softmax attention. Simulation results indicate that the linear ICL receiver attains performance comparable to standard Transformers and practical LMMSE baselines while significantly reducing the computational overhead, making it a scalable alternative for MIMO receiver design.

title: Utilization of Meteorological Data in Site Diversity Technique for Optical Satellite Communications
authors: Mai Yoshikawa, Dimitar Kolev, Ikuko Yairi, Yuma Abe, Hiroyuki Tsuji
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: In order to establish stable communications in the vertical direction between the ground and satellites, it is important to reduce the effects of weather conditions such as clouds and rainfall on communication links. As a countermeasure technology, the utilization of site diversity techniques can be mentioned as a countermeasure to avoid the effects of clouds in satellite optical communications. In this study, we examine the results of a study on the effects of optical communication based on observation data on the ground surface and meteorological data obtained from a satellite. It was found that the correlation between various observation data obtained from the environmental data information collection equipment, cloud amount, and the possibility of optical communication is high.

title: Fast Charging Techniques for Lithium-Ion Batteries: A Comparative Analysis and Algorithmic Innovations for Safety and Degradation Mitigation
authors: Surajit Middya, Bhanu Ganesh Ganta, Pradeep Kumar Yemula
abstract: The rapid proliferation of electric vehicles (EVs) and portable electronics has intensified the demand for fast, efficient, and safe lithium-ion battery charging. This paper presents a comprehensive comparative analysis of conventional and advanced charging techniques, including Constant Current-Constant Voltage (CC-CV), Multistage Constant Current (MSCC), Constant Pulse, Multiple Pulse, Pulse Burp Charging (PBC), and hybrid charging approaches. Each method is assessed based on key performance indicators such as charging speed, thermal behaviour, battery degradation, and implementation complexity.
